channel.title: NPR Topics: Culture
channel.description: Art and entertainment commentary plus interviews, book reviews, movie reviews, music reviews, comedy, and visual art. Subscribe to podcasts and follow trends in music, painting, art, architecture, photography, and design.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Wait Wait for July 18. 2026: With Not My Job guest Vicki Peterson
item.description: This week, Wait Wait is live in Chicago with guest host Tom Papa, special guest Vicki Peterson and panelists Alonzo Bodden, Helen Hong, and Dulcé Sloan
item.content:encoded:
This week, Wait Wait is live in Chicago with guest host Tom Papa, special guest Vicki Peterson and panelists Alonzo Bodden, Helen Hong, and Dulcé Sloan

(Image credit: Chelsea Guglielmino)

item.title: The 2000s called. They want their digital camera back
item.description: Why are people who weren't born 25 years ago snapping up the digital camera of that era? Blame Taylor Swift, trend cycles, childhood nostalgia and smartphone fatigue.
item.content:encoded: Why are people who weren't born 25 years ago snapping up the digital camera of that era? Blame Taylor Swift, trend cycles, childhood nostalgia and smartphone fatigue.
item.dc:creator: Alina Selyukh

item.title: This unusual, Johnny Carson-backed fund has helped Nebraska arts survive — until now
item.description: A unique public-private endowment has long shielded Nebraska's cultural scene from federal budget cuts. But a massive state deficit is putting its future in jeopardy.
item.content:encoded:
A unique public-private endowment has long shielded Nebraska's cultural scene from federal budget cuts. But a massive state deficit is putting its future in jeopardy.

(Image credit: Walker Pickering for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Chloe Veltman

item.title: D'oh! I can't believe I did that! Graceful ways to handle awkward moments
item.description: Yes, there is a way to gracefully address that you've been walking around with spinach in your teeth. Here are tactics to quickly defuse your most embarrassing, cringe-inducing moments.
item.content:encoded: Yes, there is a way to gracefully address that you've been walking around with spinach in your teeth. Here are tactics to quickly defuse your most embarrassing, cringe-inducing moments.
item.dc:creator: Malaka Gharib

item.title: Spain could make World Cup history: The first to win men's and women's trophies back-to-back
item.description: The Spanish Men's National Team will face Argentina in Sunday's World Cup final. The country's women's team lifted its first World Cup trophy in 2023.
item.content:encoded:
The Spanish Men's National Team will face Argentina in Sunday's World Cup final. The country's women's team lifted its first World Cup trophy in 2023.

(Image credit: Florencia Tan Jun)
item.dc:creator: Madeline Fox

item.title: Move over, Super Bowl? There's an even bigger – and splashier – World Cup halftime show coming
item.description: The show, which will begin somewhere around 3:45 PM ET, will air in the U.S. on Fox, Fox One and in Spanish on Telemundo, as well as on the Fox Sports app and streaming in Spanish on Peacock. It's expected to run for about 11 minutes.
item.content:encoded:
The show, which will begin somewhere around 3:45 PM ET, will air in the U.S. on Fox, Fox One and in Spanish on Telemundo, as well as on the Fox Sports app and streaming in Spanish on Peacock. It's expected to run for about 11 minutes.

(Image credit: Charly Triballeau)
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Tsioulcas

item.title: 'I Want You to Be Happy' takes on modern-day dating
item.description: In Jem Calder's debut novel, the characters have boarded a plane, baggage first -- with no idea where it will land. Will it lead to an actual relationship, nevermind happiness?
item.content:encoded: In Jem Calder's debut novel, the characters have boarded a plane, baggage first -- with no idea where it will land. Will it lead to an actual relationship, nevermind happiness?
item.dc:creator: Heller McAlpin

item.title: Photos: When the World Cup came to town
item.description: NPR member station photographers captured images of World Cup watch parties outside stadium gates, on street corners and in public parks and squares as the World Cup became, for a brief period, a part of local life.
item.content:encoded:
NPR member station photographers captured images of World Cup watch parties outside stadium gates, on street corners and in public parks and squares as the World Cup became, for a brief period, a part of local life.

(Image credit: Patricia Lim)
item.dc:creator: Meredith Nierman

item.title: David Wain follows the yellow brick road to Hollywood in his latest comedy
item.description: The Wet Hot American Summer director talks about his new film, Gail Daughtry and the Celebrity Sex Pass, and why he's still making comedies with the friends he met in college.
item.content:encoded:
The Wet Hot American Summer director talks about his new film, Gail Daughtry and the Celebrity Sex Pass, and why he's still making comedies with the friends he met in college.

(Image credit: Christina Belle)
item.dc:creator: Mandalit del Barco

item.title: 'The Odyssey' is the epic only Christopher Nolan could make — and thank Zeus he did
item.description: It's old-school, swords-and-sandals cinema, yet still modern and richly satisfying.
item.content:encoded:
It's old-school, swords-and-sandals cinema, yet still modern and richly satisfying.

(Image credit: Melinda Sue Gordon)
item.dc:creator: Glen Weldon
